item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded: I knew it felt familiar, but I couldn’t remember the name of it. You did a good job of reimagining it to fit in the SCP universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this featured, it was one of my favs from the cliche contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even for a cliche, this is actually pretty interesting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's not just a monster that kills people. It's a monster who sometimes helps out around the farm at night, but other times destroys the farm. It's also got a weird kind of intelligence. Like, it doesn't notice staff rotating in and out, but it can always tell when someone is entering or exiting a 400 hectare area? Also it respects property lines? There's some logical holes here, but hey, it's a spooky monster and I liked the overall story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's, like… the anti-Totoro? Just the right level of spook, and it's not entirely a straight no-frills SSMM because ultimately it seems to be an eldritch farmer just trying to grow a stand of trees, and maybe not understanding that it's doing any harm by picking up some of those strange squishy pink and brown creatures that live in cute little huts dotted around the places it wants to farm and using them as grow-bags for tree seeds. But it is now somewhat annoyed and confused about why the other creatures uprooted the new saplings and took them away and has decided to study them more closely, whilst harvesting a few of the other animals that live in the same clearing to start a new nursery with…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image I have in my head of a pale, skinny creature climbing a tree and placing a dream catcher on it with one small hand and one massive, sharp hand is enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I don't recall downvoting this. Time for a rerate.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
So this is my entry for Cliche-Con, A Spooky Scary Murder Monster. I play it pretty straight, there's nothing here that really elevates it above the trope, but I hope it's at least mildly interesting.

This was inspired by a creepypasta, The Thing That Stalks the Fields. It's not a particularly good creepypasta, but for some reason it stands out in my memory.

Thanks to everyone who looked over this in chat and on the forum.
